steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers concluded that septum M23 formed at 14.0°C (colder) and septum F02 formed at 20.8°C (warmer). The logic is: deeper water $\Rightarrow$ lower temperature AND higher oxygen-18. So M23 (colder = deeper) should have more oxygen-18 than F02 (warmer = shallower). A finding that weakens this conclusion must contradict that expected oxygen-18 pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — At a depth of 140 meters in the nautilids' habitat, the water is 20.8°C and the concentration of oxygen-18 is equal to that in sample F02.: This is consistent with F02 forming at 20.8°C at a shallow depth — it supports the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice B — The concentration of oxygen-18 in F02 is higher than that in M23.: M23 is colder, so it should have more oxygen-18 than the warmer F02. If F02 actually has higher oxygen-18, the isotopic signature does not match the predicted temperature relationship — this directly undermines the basis for the researchers' temperature conclusions. ✓
  • Choice C — The concentration of oxygen-18 in F02 is lower than that in M23.: This is exactly what the passage's logic predicts (warmer F02 = shallower = less oxygen-18). It supports the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice D — At a depth of 385 meters in the nautilids' habitat, the water is 14.0°C and the concentration of oxygen-18 is equal to that in sample M23.: This is consistent with M23 forming at 14.0°C at a deeper, colder depth — it supports the conclusion. ✗
That matches Choice B — The concentration of oxygen-18 in F02 is higher than that in M23..
